_comment: Reference identities for HF basis-ctx campaign test 1c (A-12 fold).
Each entry asserts an LHS expansion = expected basis-polynomial form.
Verified by classical Euler / Zagier / Bigotte-Jacob-Petitot MZV
reduction identities. The C++ test compares the FLINT canonical
to_string() of expansion[lhs] against the canonical to_string() of
Rat::parse(basis_ctx, expected), so any equivalent canonical form is
accepted (no whitespace / ordering sensitivity beyond FLINT's own
Source / convention: HyperIntica's mzvAllReductions table at weight
<= 8, classical Euler identities for even Zeta, depth-2 reductions
Algebraic (vs numerical) approach justified: HF stores Rats as
fmpq_mpoly canonical forms; comparing canonical strings is exact
and dependency-free. Numerical evaluation at high precision via
mpmath would add a dep without strengthening the check.

identities.comment: Zagier depth-2 weight-5 reduction; complementary to mzv_2_3
